phi_warning: NO PHI IN FAQ - Generic healthcare guidance only

entries.question: How do I schedule an appointment?
entries.answer: You can schedule appointments through our patient portal, mobile app, or by calling our scheduling line. Online scheduling is available 24/7. Select your preferred provider, date, and time. You'll receive a confirmation email and text reminder.

entries.question: How do I cancel or reschedule my appointment?
entries.answer: Log into the patient portal, go to 'My Appointments', and select 'Cancel' or 'Reschedule'. Please provide at least 24 hours notice. You can also call our scheduling line or use the mobile app.

entries.question: What should I bring to my appointment?
entries.answer: Please bring: valid photo ID, insurance card, list of current medications, list of allergies, any referral forms, and copayment if applicable. New patients should also complete the new patient forms available on our portal.

entries.question: How early should I arrive for my appointment?
entries.answer: Arrive 15 minutes early for established patients, 30 minutes early for new patients. This allows time for check-in and any necessary paperwork. Telehealth appointments require no travel time.

entries.question: Do you offer same-day appointments?
entries.answer: Yes, same-day appointments are available for urgent but non-emergency issues. Call our scheduling line when we open or check the patient portal for same-day availability. Emergency situations should go to the ER.

entries.question: How can I pay my medical bill?
entries.answer: Pay online through the patient portal, by mail with the payment coupon, by phone at our billing number, or in person at any location. We accept credit/debit cards, checks, and HSA/FSA cards.

entries.question: Do you offer payment plans?
entries.answer: Yes, we offer interest-free payment plans for qualifying balances. Contact our billing department to set up a plan. We also offer financial assistance for patients who qualify based on income.

entries.question: Why did I receive a bill after my insurance paid?
entries.answer: The bill is likely for your portion: deductible, copay, or coinsurance. Review your Explanation of Benefits (EOB) from your insurance. If you believe there's an error, contact our billing department.

entries.question: How do I get an itemized bill?
entries.answer: Request an itemized bill through the patient portal under 'Billing' > 'Request Itemized Statement' or call our billing department. Itemized bills are typically available within 5 business days.

entries.question: What insurance plans do you accept?
entries.answer: We accept most major insurance plans including Blue Cross Blue Shield, Aetna, Cigna, United Healthcare, Medicare, and Medicaid. Contact our insurance verification line to confirm your specific plan is accepted.

entries.question: How do I update my insurance information?
entries.answer: Update your insurance through the patient portal under 'Profile' > 'Insurance' or bring your new insurance card to your next appointment. Notify us at least 48 hours before appointments.

entries.question: What is a prior authorization and do I need one?
entries.answer: Prior authorization is approval from your insurance before certain services. It may be required for specialty referrals, certain procedures, imaging, or expensive medications. Our staff will verify and obtain authorizations.

entries.question: How do I get a referral to a specialist?
entries.answer: Schedule an appointment with your primary care provider who will evaluate and provide a referral if needed. Some insurance plans require PCP referrals for specialists. Referrals are sent directly to the specialist.

entries.question: How do I request a prescription refill?
entries.answer: Request refills through the patient portal, mobile app, or contact your pharmacy. Allow 48-72 hours for processing. Some medications require an appointment for renewal. Controlled substances require an office visit.

entries.question: Can I get a 90-day supply of my medication?
entries.answer: Many maintenance medications are available in 90-day supplies. Request through your pharmacy or patient portal. Some insurance plans prefer mail-order pharmacies for 90-day supplies at lower cost.

entries.question: What if my medication is not covered by insurance?
entries.answer: Contact our office - we can request a prior authorization, suggest alternatives, or provide manufacturer coupons. Many pharmacies offer discount programs. Our pharmacists can help find affordable options.

entries.question: How do I transfer my prescription to a different pharmacy?
entries.answer: Contact your new pharmacy and provide your prescription information. They will handle the transfer. Or request through our patient portal. Controlled substance transfers have additional requirements.

entries.question: How do I request my medical records?
entries.answer: Submit a request through the patient portal under 'Medical Records' > 'Request Records' or complete a release form at any location. Records are typically available within 5-7 business days. There may be a fee for paper copies.

entries.question: How do I transfer my records to a new provider?
entries.answer: Complete an authorization form with your new provider or submit a request through our patient portal. Include your new provider's contact information. We will send records directly to them.

entries.question: Can I access my test results online?
entries.answer: Yes, most test results are available in the patient portal within 24-72 hours. You'll receive a notification when results are ready. Some sensitive results may require a provider discussion before release.

entries.question: How long do you keep medical records?
entries.answer: We retain medical records for 7 years for adults, and until age 21 or 7 years (whichever is longer) for minors. This meets HIPAA and state requirements. Records are stored securely with encryption.

entries.question: How do I schedule a telehealth appointment?
entries.answer: Schedule through the patient portal or app by selecting 'Telehealth' as the visit type. You'll receive a link to join your video appointment. Ensure you have a stable internet connection and private space.

entries.question: What technology do I need for a telehealth visit?
entries.answer: You need a smartphone, tablet, or computer with a camera and microphone. Use Chrome, Safari, or the mobile app. Test your connection before the appointment. Find a quiet, private location.

entries.question: What can be treated via telehealth?
entries.answer: Telehealth is appropriate for many concerns: follow-ups, medication management, minor illnesses, mental health, chronic disease management, and consultations. Physical exams require in-person visits.

entries.question: Is telehealth covered by insurance?
entries.answer: Most insurance plans cover telehealth visits, often with the same copay as in-person visits. Medicare and Medicaid also cover telehealth. Verify coverage with your insurance if unsure.

entries.question: Do you offer after-hours care?
entries.answer: Yes, we offer after-hours telehealth for urgent but non-emergency issues. Call our main line to be connected. For medical emergencies, call 911 or go to the nearest emergency room.

entries.question: What is your no-show policy?
entries.answer: We request 24-hour notice for cancellations. Repeated no-shows may result in a fee or discharge from the practice. We understand emergencies happen - please call as soon as possible.

entries.question: Do you offer a self-pay discount?
entries.answer: Yes, patients without insurance receive a discount on services. Ask about our self-pay rates when scheduling. We also offer transparent pricing for common services.

entries.question: How do I correct an error in my medical records?
entries.answer: Submit a written request through the patient portal specifying the error and correction needed. Our medical records team will review and make appropriate amendments. You'll be notified of the outcome.

entries.question: Can I have someone with me during a telehealth visit?
entries.answer: Yes, you may have a family member or caregiver present. Position the camera so the provider can see both of you if needed. This can be helpful for discussing care plans.
